touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn connection request sent to Ethan Kapoor, VP of Sales at JadeEngine. No response received.
Initial cold email sent to Ethan highlighting rep productivity gains through automation. No response received.
Follow-up email with case study from similar healthcare IT company. Ethan responded expressing interest in learning more about automation capabilities for his sales team.

questions_asked.question: What kind of automation are we talking about here?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited workflow automation including data entry, follow-ups, task creation, and CRM updates - all the manual work that slows your reps down.

touches.ai_summary: Email exchange to qualify fit and schedule technical eval. Ethan confirmed team size (9 reps) and main pain point around manual CRM data entry eating up selling time.

questions_asked.question: How much time would you estimate your reps spend on administrative tasks vs. actual selling?
questions_asked.answer: Probably 40% admin, 60% selling. We're growing fast and need them focused on prospects, not paperwork.

next_steps: Technical evaluation scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: 43-minute technical evaluation call with Ethan. Walked through automation workflows, CRM integration, and rep productivity dashboard. Ethan engaged and asking detailed questions about implementation timeline and user adoption.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-3 weeks from contract signature to full rollout, including training.

questions_asked.question: What's the learning curve like for reps who aren't super technical?
questions_asked.answer: Very minimal - most reps are fully productive within the first week. The interface is designed to work alongside existing workflows.

questions_asked.question: Do you have buy-in from leadership on investing in sales productivity tools?
questions_asked.answer: I have budget authority for my team's tools. This would come out of my sales ops budget.

objections.objection: We need to see this work in our specific workflow before committing.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with technical eval recording, ROI calculator showing potential 15-hour monthly savings per rep, and availability for full demo. Ethan confirmed interest and requested demo for following week.
next_steps: Demo scheduled with Ethan and 2 sales reps

touches.ai_summary: 32-minute product demo with Ethan and two sales reps. Demonstrated live automation workflows, Salesforce integration, and reporting dashboard. Reps responded positively to ease of use. Ethan focused on pricing and contract terms.

questions_asked.question: Can you walk through the pricing tiers again? What's included in each?
questions_asked.answer: Standard tier is $1,500/seat with core automation. Unlimited tier at $1,200/seat includes everything - unlimited workflows, advanced analytics, priority support.

questions_asked.question: What kind of contract terms are we looking at?
questions_asked.answer: Standard is annual commitment. We can discuss quarterly for the right fit.

questions_asked.question: Would you want to start with the full team or pilot with a few reps first?
questions_asked.answer: Full team - if we're doing this, I want everyone on it from day one. Pilots just delay value.

next_steps: Send formal proposal for 9 seats unlimited tier

touches.ai_summary: 22-minute proposal review call. Discussed $10,800 list price for 9 seats unlimited tier. Ethan pushed back on pricing, citing budget constraints and request for discount to hit his Q2 numbers.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on pricing if we commit today?
questions_asked.answer: Let me talk to my manager about what we can do for an end-of-quarter close.

objections.objection: The $10,800 is above what I budgeted. I was thinking closer to $7-8k to make this work this quarter.

next_steps: Check with leadership on discount approval
Revised proposal by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Email with revised proposal at $7,500 (27.5% discount) contingent on close by month-end. Ethan confirmed pricing works and requested one final call to review contract terms and get internal signoff.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly billing instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can accommodate quarterly billing at this price point.

touches.ai_summary: 27-minute executive alignment call with Ethan. Reviewed contract terms, implementation timeline, and success metrics. Ethan verbally committed to moving forward pending legal review. No other executives joined the call despite invitation.

questions_asked.question: Will we have a chance to meet with your CFO or CEO before contract signature?
questions_asked.answer: Not necessary - I own this decision and have the authority to sign. I'll loop them in during implementation.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can we get the contract over for legal review?
questions_asked.answer: I'll send it over today. Our legal team is pretty fast.

next_steps: Target signature by end of week
